summary: SAP S/4HANA connector — incremental delta sync via OData change tokens
description_md:
As a end-user, I want the SAP S/4HANA connector to support incremental delta sync via OData change tokens so that I can efficiently synchronize only changed records without full data extraction.

The current full extraction approach consumes excessive bandwidth and API quota for large SAP S/4HANA datasets. OData change tokens enable the connector to track and retrieve only delta changes since the last successful sync, significantly improving performance for high-volume data sources.

**Acceptance Criteria**:

1. Connector successfully retrieves OData change token from initial full extraction and persists it in the connector metadata store.
2. Delta sync operations use the stored change token to fetch only records modified since the last sync timestamp.
3. Change token is automatically refreshed and updated after each successful delta sync operation.
4. Sync history logs accurately record the change token values, record counts, and timestamps for each delta sync run.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implemented OData change token support using the `$deltaLink` and `$deltaToken` query options. Tested with SAP S/4HANA Cloud sandbox - delta sync reduced payload size by 94% compared to full extraction.

comments.author: qa1@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: QA passed. Verified: (1) change token persisted correctly in connector metadata, (2) delta sync only returns modified records, (3) token refresh works across multiple sync cycles. Ready for release in v2.3.
